[
  {"factor": "GHG Emissions", "grade": "VAGUE", "evidence": "From 2023 all vessels over 400 gross tonnage became subject to IMO requirements as to energy efficiency design (EEXI) and the carbon intensity of operations (CII)."},
  {"factor": "Air Quality", "grade": "QUANTIFIED", "evidence": "A global sulphur cap of 0.5% applies to marine fuel under MARPOL with a stricter 0.1% cap in environmentally sensitive areas."},
  {"factor": "Water & Wastewater Management", "grade": "VAGUE", "evidence": "The Clean Water Act regulates wastewater and other effluent discharges from bp's facilities, and bp is required to obtain discharge permits, install control equipment and implement operational controls and preventative measures."},
  {"factor": "Ecological Impacts", "grade": "VAGUE", "evidence": "The Endangered Species Act (ESA) and Marine Mammal Protection Act protect certain species' habitats from adverse human impacts by restricting operations or development at certain times and in certain places."},
  {"factor": "Human Rights & Community Relations", "grade": "VAGUE", "evidence": "The Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive (CSDDD) entered into force in July 2024 but has undergone targeted amendments as part of the Omnibus package. It still requires certain EU and non-EU companies to conduct due diligence on human rights and environmental risks"},
  {"factor": "Employee Health & Safety", "grade": "VAGUE", "evidence": "The Occupational Safety and Health Act imposes workplace safety and health requirements on bp operations along with significant process safety management obligations, requiring continuous evaluation and improvement of operational practices to enhance safety and reduce workplace emissions at gas processing, refining and other regulated facilities."},
  {"factor": "Business Model Resilience", "grade": "UNDISCLOSED", "evidence": null},
  {"factor": "Business Ethics", "grade": "UNDISCLOSED", "evidence": null},
  {"factor": "Management of the Legal & Regulatory Environment", "grade": "VAGUE", "evidence": "A number of pending or anticipated governmental proceedings against certain bp group companies under environmental laws could result in monetary or other sanctions. Group companies are also subject to environmental claims for personal injury and property damage alleging the release of, or exposure to, hazardous substances."},
  {"factor": "Critical Incident Risk Management", "grade": "QUANTIFIED", "evidence": "To meet its financial responsibility requirements, bp shipping maintains marine oil pollution liability insurance in respect of its operated ships to a maximum limit of $1 billion for each occurrence through mutual insurance associations (P&I Clubs)"}
